name: Box
description: Cloud content management and collaboration platform. Yields shared link and collaboration policy evidence, Shield threat detection alerts, enterprise admin event logs, user activity reports and retention policy configuration.

steps.action: Navigate to Admin Console > Enterprise Settings > Content & Sharing and capture a screenshot of the Shared Links section (default link access, 'invited people only' restrictions, expiry defaults)
steps.audit_note: Include the browser URL and system clock in the screenshot so the capture date is verifiable

steps.action: Navigate to Admin Console > Reports > Create Report > User Activity, tick the Shared Links and Collaboration action types, set the date range for the collection period and select Export
steps.permissions_required: Admin, or Co-Admin with 'Run new reports and access existing reports'
steps.audit_note: The export is written as a CSV into the Box Reports folder; ensure date ranges between collections leave no gaps

steps.action: Navigate to Admin Console > Shield > Dashboard, review open threat detection alerts for the period and export or screenshot the alert list
steps.audit_note: If your enterprise does not licence Box Shield, note that in the evidence record and rely on the User Activity report for anomaly review

steps.action: Navigate to Admin Console > Governance > Retention and capture the list of retention policies, their durations and assignments
steps.audit_note: Retention policy configuration is point-in-time evidence — capture it each period even if unchanged

steps.action: Download the CSVs from the Box Reports folder and upload them with the screenshots to the evidence repository using date-stamped filenames and evidence ID references
steps.security_note: Reports contain user names, email addresses and file paths — restrict access to the evidence repository and do not share the Box Reports folder broadly

steps.action: Navigate to Admin Console > Reports > Create Report > User Activity and configure the columns, action types (shared links, collaboration) and filters to match your evidence requirement
steps.permissions_required: Admin, or Co-Admin with 'Run new reports and access existing reports'

steps.action: Enable 'Save these settings to use in the future', then select Schedule report and set the recurrence (weekly, monthly or yearly), start date and end date
steps.audit_note: Scheduled runs deposit a fresh CSV in the Box Reports folder on each cycle with Box's generation timestamp

steps.action: Note that Security Logs and Outbound Activity reports cannot currently be scheduled — collect those on the L1 manual cadence or via the Events API (L3)
steps.audit_note: Record this limitation in your collection procedure so auditors understand why those reports are gathered differently

steps.action: Restrict the Box Reports folder to the compliance team and add a collaboration on it for your evidence tooling or shared compliance account
steps.security_note: Scheduled report output inherits Box Reports folder permissions — audit its collaborator list so report data is not exposed enterprise-wide

steps.action: Each cycle, move the generated CSV from the Box Reports folder into the evidence repository and confirm the run completed with status 'Complete'
steps.audit_note: Verify the first two scheduled runs arrive as expected, then spot-check monthly for skipped runs

steps.action: In the Box Developer Console, create a Platform App using Server Authentication (Client Credentials Grant) or JWT, then generate credentials — a client ID/secret for CCG, or a public/private keypair for JWT
steps.permissions_required: Any user can create the app; a Box Admin or Co-Admin must authorise it
steps.security_note: Store the client secret or JWT private key in a secrets manager, never in source control. The app runs as a service account, not a personal login.

steps.action: In the app's Configuration tab, enable the 'Manage enterprise properties' scope so the service account can read enterprise events, then have a Box Admin or Co-Admin authorise the app from the Admin Console
steps.permissions_required: Admin or Co-Admin to authorise the app
steps.audit_note: App authorisations and scope changes are themselves recorded in the enterprise event stream

steps.action: Call GET https://api.box.com/2.0/events?stream_type=admin_logs with created_after/created_before filters to collect the enterprise admin event history (shared link creation, collaboration changes, downloads, logins)
steps.permissions_required: Service account token from the authorised app
steps.audit_note: admin_logs favours completeness: events are chronological, de-duplicated and retained for up to one year — the right stream for audit evidence

steps.action: Also collect configuration evidence on the same schedule: GET https://api.box.com/2.0/retention_policies for retention policy state (requires Box Governance and the retention scopes enabled by Box support)
steps.audit_note: Pairing event evidence with configuration snapshots shows both that controls are set and that they were not changed unnoticed

steps.action: Schedule the collection daily via cron or a CI/CD pipeline, persisting the next_stream_position between runs, with error handling that logs failures and alerts the compliance team
steps.security_note: Rotate the client secret or JWT keypair on your credential rotation schedule and remove the app authorisation if it is decommissioned
steps.audit_note: Persisting the stream position guarantees consecutive runs leave no gaps

steps.action: Validate each run by checking the daily event count against baseline and confirming event timestamps are contiguous with the previous run — flag anomalies for investigation
steps.audit_note: Volume and continuity validation provide assurance of collection completeness

L4.title: Box Managed Evidence Pipeline with Event Streaming

steps.action: Stream near real-time enterprise events by long-polling GET https://api.box.com/2.0/events?stream_type=admin_logs_streaming into your SIEM or evidence platform
steps.permissions_required: Authorised Platform App service account (JWT or Client Credentials Grant)
steps.audit_note: admin_logs_streaming favours low latency: events may arrive out of order or duplicated and only two weeks are retained — de-duplicate on event_id at ingestion

steps.action: Ingest Box Shield alerts through the same stream — Shield alert events (e.g., suspicious locations, anomalous downloads, malicious content) appear in the enterprise event stream as SHIELD_ALERT events
steps.audit_note: Streaming Shield alerts into the SIEM turns detections into retained, timestamped evidence rather than transient dashboard entries

steps.action: Run a daily reconciliation job that replays the same window from stream_type=admin_logs (the complete, chronological feed) and compares counts against what the streaming feed delivered
steps.audit_note: Reconciling the low-latency stream against the completeness-oriented history feed is what makes the pipeline defensible as gap-free

steps.action: Snapshot control configuration continuously: nightly pulls of Enterprise Settings evidence (shared link policy via scheduled reports) and GET /2.0/retention_policies, diffed against the prior snapshot with alerts on change
steps.security_note: Alert on any weakening of shared link defaults (e.g., open links re-enabled) as a potential control failure, not just a config change

steps.action: Publish a collection-health dashboard: events/day per stream, reconciliation mismatches, Shield alerts ingested vs raised, stream position lag and failed runs, with alerting on gaps
steps.audit_note: The dashboard itself is continuous evidence of a managed collection process

steps.action: Run an automated quarterly review that produces a collection-health report, verifies credential rotation for the Platform App, and re-confirms the app's scopes and authorisation are still minimal
steps.security_note: Include a check of the Box Reports folder collaborator list and the SIEM ingestion endpoint's access controls
